channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Hefty Branch Falls near Busy Path, 29th April
item.description:
A sprawling beach tree shed a huge branch in the strong winds today - just missing a very well used public pathway over Broomhill Common and causing damage to my fencing. Luckily, no serious damage was caused on this occasion but a stark warning that the tree requires urgent management to avoid any future catastrophe to people and property. In addition, the adjacent dead tree covered by overgrown Ivy sways alarmingly in strong winds too further endangering people, highways and property.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wiltshire Road, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branches fallen, 29th April
item.description:
Large tree branches has fallen off the tree and is going straight across the pavement. Blocking the pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 127, St Paul's Wood Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kerb has been dislodged by contractors LGVs, 29th April
item.description:
Thames Water have been carrying out water mains replacement in Basing Drive, Bexley DA51EP. To do this they have needed to drive various heavy goods vehicles over the kerb on the corner outside number 15 Basing Drive DA51EP to get around the bend in the road. This has dislodged and broken the kerb stone, this is now laying in the road where vehicles could collide with it and pedestrians could fall over the kerb.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Basing Drive, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown willows causing damage, 29th April
item.description:
I am reporting these large willow trees because they have become severely overgrown and appear to have been unmanaged for a long time despite this being raised multiple times previously. The trees are causing several serious issues: Large roots are spreading under nearby properties and appear to be contributing to damage to surrounding houses. The roots are lifting and sinking the road surface, creating an uneven and hazardous roadway. Branches are regularly falling from the trees and have already fallen onto parked vehicles. The canopy is excessively overgrown and poses an ongoing safety risk to residents, pedestrians, and cars. These trees require urgent inspection and proper maintenance, including pruning and assessment of the root damage before the situation becomes worse. Please investigate this matter as soon as possible.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Crouch Croft, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vandalised tree., 29th April
item.description:
This tree was vandalised a few weeks ago by an amateur with a chainsaw. Can it be saved? Cut off branches can be seen piled up on opposite side of road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bicknor Road, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge has been left by the car parking spaces - on the side for local pub / businesses to park, 29th April
item.description:
It is leaking onto the road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Jago Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees & Shrubbery Overgrown onto the pavement, blocking bus stop shelter and flagpole., 29th April
item.description:
The shrubbery has overgrown this bus stop for over a year now. There is litter everywhere and has a horrible odour to it. This is just under the railway bridge surrounding the bus stop.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waterfall Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bus hit and turned around so traffic can’t read, 29th April
item.description:
A bus hit it in passing this evening turning the sign around so traffic can’t read the sign

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beck Lane, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rapidly deepening pothole., 29th April
item.description:
Started at least 6 months ago, now getting much worse. Difficult to avoid when cars are parked both sides of road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Irene Road, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large plants falling and completely blocking the road., 29th April
item.description:
Two plants are falling away from the council- controlled and maintained verge. These are making the road impassable and it is dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tilson Close, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole, 29th April
item.description:
There is a large pothole in the road outside no.8 Harefield Road. When the 484 bus goes down the road and hits the pothole we can feel our house vibrate. This is especially common at night as the drivers do not see it in the dark and always hit it with their offside wheels. This hole is getting worse, we first started feeling the vibration about a year ago.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Harefield Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken covers to underground cables, 29th April
item.description:
Virgin media put orange barriers around broken manhole covers many months ago which keep falling down- but no one came back to fix it You can see through it and a person could trip and fall and even fall through it potentially
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parsonage Manorway, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Rubbish….again!, 29th April
item.description:
Another pile of rubbish has been left on the corner of Suffolk Place. Looks like a couple of pallets plus other bagged stuff.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commonwealth Way, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 29th April
item.description:
Fly tipping by the road just inside the wood
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Knee Hill, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: North pavement White chair, 29th April
item.description:
Whote chair on north pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warwick Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large branch from tree north pavement, 29th April
item.description:
Large branch on pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warwick Road, Anerley, Greater London

item.title: Broken tree cage, 29th April
item.description:
The surrounding cage snd support to the this tree is broken

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alkham Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Hoover on pavement, 29th April
item.description:
Hoover on pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warwick Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown hedges, weeds and stinging nettles, 29th April
item.description:
Extremely dangerous overgrowth. Dangerous for children walking down pathway and now almost impossible to get a pram down it. There is no way a wheelchair user could use it - the pathway needs widening and levelling too but the immediate issue is the stinging nettles and overgrown greenery.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Orchard Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass cutting, 29th April
item.description:
The grass in Abbey Wood Park and in Mountjoy Close & Andwell Close has only been cut once this season and now has foot-tall weeds. It should be cut immediately.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Andwell Close, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet
